scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, re-reviewed against the live v3 API reference (developers.kit.com/api-reference/v3/*, fetched 2026-07-04; the v3 API is deprecated in favor of v4 but Kit's own docs confirm v3 remains live and documented, and this bundle's api_secret-query-param auth is v3-specific and not compatible with v4's API-key-header/OAuth2 scheme, so this expansion stays within v3 rather than rewriting the connector onto an incompatible auth model). 7 read streams (the 5 wave1-pilot streams retained, plus custom_fields/purchases) and 12 write actions covering subscriber/tag/form/sequence/broadcast/custom-field/purchase/webhook lifecycle mutations. Every endpoint below is covered_by a stream/write action XOR excluded with a real category and reason. Exclusions favor breadth of real mutations over: subscription-list sub-resource reads that duplicate the subscribers stream's record shape filtered a different way (duplicate_of); the account endpoint (non_data_endpoint, an account metadata snapshot); and the one genuinely destructive global mutation (unsubscribe, destructive_admin).

excluded.reason: single-subscriber detail; identical record shape to the subscribers list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
per-subscriber tag list; identical record shape to the global tags stream, just filtered to one subscriber

globally unsubscribes an email address from every form/sequence/tag in the account at once; irreversible-in-effect (Kit requires manual resubscribe) marketing-consent action, no reverse-ETL use case for this port

breadth-vs-cost triage: remove_tag_from_subscriber (the ID-addressed DELETE variant) already covers tag removal; this email-addressed POST variant is a duplicate mutation path, not a distinct capability
per-tag subscriber list; identical record shape to the global subscribers stream, just filtered to one tag
per-form subscriber list; identical record shape to the global subscribers stream, just filtered to one form
per-sequence subscriber list; identical record shape to the global subscribers stream, just filtered to one sequence
single-broadcast detail; identical record shape to the broadcasts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
engagement-metrics snapshot (opens/clicks/unsubscribes) for one broadcast, not a syncable object list
permanently removes the field AND all associated subscriber data for every subscriber (per Kit's own docs), no reverse-ETL use case for this port
single-purchase detail; identical record shape to the purchases list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
account metadata snapshot (name, primary email), not a syncable object stream
